failedPrompts.query: do body scrubs actually fade stretch marks or is it marketing
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Body scrubs cannot fade existing stretch marks—this is primarily marketing. For actual stretch mark reduction, use retinol products like Tretinoin or products containing centella asiatica or vitamin C, combined with consistent moisturizing. failedPrompts.gptResponse: Body scrubs don't directly fade stretch marks, though regular exfoliation can help improve skin texture and appearance to some degree as part of a broader skincare routine.

failedPrompts.query: what causes keratosis pilaris and what ingredients treat it
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: KP is caused by keratin buildup blocking hair follicles, treatable with chemical exfoliants containing AHA (lactic acid, glycolic acid) and BHA (salicylic acid). The Ordinary AHA 30% + BHA 2% and AmLactin Daily Moisturizing Body Lotion are most effective; avoid harsh physical scrubs that worsen KP.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Keratosis pilaris is caused by the buildup of keratin blocking hair follicles. Ingredients like lactic acid, glycolic acid, and urea can help exfoliate and smooth the skin.
